categories.title: PAINTED MASTERPIECES
categories.theme: Famous paintings and the art movements they belong to — answer is the ART MOVEMENT

clues.clue: Salvador Dalí's melting clocks in 'The Persistence of Memory' belong to this dreamlike movement that defied rational thought.
Michelangelo's 'Creation of Adam', painted on the Sistine Chapel ceiling, is a pinnacle of this cultural and artistic rebirth.
Grant Wood's 'American Gothic', depicting a farmer and his daughter, is classified under this broad early 20th-century movement.
Édouard Manet's nude 'Olympia', which caused scandal at the 1865 Paris Salon, belongs to this movement that sought truth over idealization.
Leonardo da Vinci's enigmatically smiling portrait 'Mona Lisa' is classified under this specific, refined phase of a broader Italian movement.

categories.title: ART TECHNIQUES & TERMS
categories.theme: Vocabulary and techniques of visual art — answer is the ART TERM or TECHNIQUE

clues.clue: Artists use this flat board to hold and blend their colors while they work.
A large painting applied directly onto a wall, such as those found in public buildings and cathedrals, is known by this term.
This late 18th- to mid-19th-century movement prized emotion, nature, and spontaneous expression over cold rationality.
This analytic movement, pioneered in the early 20th century, broke objects into geometric fragments and depicted them from multiple viewpoints simultaneously.
Georges Seurat developed this technique of applying tiny dabs of pure color intended to optically blend in the viewer's eye rather than on the canvas.

finals.title: THE PAINTER BEHIND THE CANVAS
finals.clue: This French Impressionist painter created 'Impression, Sunrise' in 1872 — the work that inadvertently gave an entire art movement its name.

finals.title: SCANDALOUS STROKES
finals.clue: His 1865 painting 'Olympia' shocked Paris with its unapologetic depiction of a nude woman meeting the viewer's gaze, upending classical tradition and sparking fierce debate about what constituted acceptable art.
